mission.title: Install and Configure Kubevirt on Kubernetes
mission.description: KubeVirt is a virtual machine management add-on for Kubernetes that allows you to define and manage virtual machines alongside other Kubernetes resources. Installing KubeVirt enables you to leverage Kubernetes for virtualization, providing a unified platform for managing both containerized and virtualized workloads.

steps.title: Add the KubeVirt Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the KubeVirt Helm repository to your local Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add kubevirt https://charts.kubevirt.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install KubeVirt using Helm
steps.description:
Install KubeVirt in the 'kubevirt' namespace (it will be created if it doesn't exist) with the following command:
```bash
helm install kubevirt kubevirt/kubevirt --namespace kubevirt --create-namespace --version v1.7.0
```

steps.title: Verify KubeVirt installation
steps.description:
Check that all KubeVirt components are running by executing:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevirt
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for KubeVirt components
steps.description:
To ensure optimal performance, you can configure resource limits for KubeVirt components. Create a YAML file named `kubevirt-resources.yaml` with the following content:
```yaml
apiVersion: kubevirt.io/v1
kind: KubeVirt
metadata:
  name: kubevirt
spec:
  configuration:
    developerMode: false
    resourceLimits:
      cpu: "2"
      memory: "4Gi"
``` 
Then apply the configuration:
```bash
kubectl apply -f kubevirt-resources.yaml -n kubevirt
```

steps.title: Enable monitoring for KubeVirt
steps.description:
To enable monitoring, you can install Prometheus and Grafana. Use the following command to install them:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubevirt/kubevirt/master/deploy/monitoring/monitoring.yaml
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of KubeVirt will show all pods in the 'kubevirt' namespace as running when you execute `kubectl get pods -n kubevirt`. You will also have configured resource limits and enabled monitoring for your KubeVirt installation.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kubevirt https://charts.kubevirt.io
helm repo update
helm install kubevirt kubevirt/kubevirt --namespace kubevirt --create-namespace --version v1.7.0

apiVersion: kubevirt.io/v1
kind: KubeVirt
metadata:
  name: kubevirt
spec:
  configuration:
    developerMode: false
    resourceLimits:
      cpu: "2"
      memory: "4Gi"

prerequisites.description: You need a running Kubernetes cluster and the Helm and kubectl command-line tools installed.
